objective: Repair MCP directory drift so ThumbGate discovery points to one canonical identity and one proof-backed install path.
headline: Fix legacy-name MCP directory drift before scaling discovery.
shortDescription: ThumbGate already has live MCP directory discovery, but major surfaces still leak retired names and old repo paths. Repair those first, then scale directory acquisition.
summary: Current checks show one canonical listing on MCP.so, two legacy-name directory results on Glama and Smithery, one legacy repo entry on the highest-reach awesome list, and one missing awesome-list entry.

surfaces.name: MCP.so canonical listing
surfaces.role: Live discovery surface with the current ThumbGate slug.
surfaces.operatorUse: Use as the reference listing while repairing drift everywhere else.
surfaces.evidenceSummary: Direct curl check confirmed the page title `Thumbgate MCP Server`, current ThumbGate overview copy, and the canonical GitHub link.
surfaces.nextRepair: Keep description and proof links aligned with `COMMERCIAL_TRUTH.md` and `VERIFICATION_EVIDENCE.md` as the canonical directory copy.

surfaces.name: Glama search result
surfaces.role: High-volume MCP registry search surface that still leaks legacy naming.
surfaces.publicStatus: Search for `thumbgate` resolves to the legacy slug `IgorGanapolsky/mcp-memory-gateway`.
surfaces.operatorUse: Repair the public slug, summary, and package naming before pushing more Glama-facing discovery.
surfaces.evidenceSummary: Search HTML exposes `ThumbGate` as the display name but still points to the legacy `mcp-memory-gateway` slug and legacy plain-text description.
surfaces.nextRepair: Claim or update the listing so the slug, repo name, and summary are ThumbGate-only and no longer mention the old gateway positioning.

surfaces.name: Smithery search result
surfaces.role: Installer-facing directory surface with a legacy namespace result.
surfaces.publicStatus: Search returns `rlhf-loop/thumbgate` with `0 connections` instead of a canonical ThumbGate namespace.
surfaces.operatorUse: Publish or repair the canonical Smithery listing before treating Smithery as an active acquisition lane.
surfaces.evidenceSummary: Direct search output shows `thumbgate [remote]`, the legacy `rlhf-loop/thumbgate` namespace, and a details link at the legacy path.
surfaces.nextRepair: Publish or migrate Smithery metadata to a canonical ThumbGate namespace and retire the legacy `rlhf-loop` ownership path.

surfaces.name: punkpeye awesome-mcp-servers
surfaces.role: Largest GitHub awesome-list discovery surface in the current repo research.
surfaces.publicStatus: Listed, but under the legacy repository `IgorGanapolsky/mcp-memory-gateway`.
surfaces.operatorUse: Open a repair PR that swaps the repo name and keeps the description ThumbGate-only.
surfaces.evidenceSummary: README search returns a live entry, but it still points to `IgorGanapolsky/mcp-memory-gateway` instead of `IgorGanapolsky/ThumbGate`.
surfaces.nextRepair: Submit a PR replacing the legacy repo path with the ThumbGate repo while keeping the pre-action gates description.

surfaces.name: appcypher awesome-mcp-servers
surfaces.role: Secondary GitHub discovery list that currently has no ThumbGate entry.
surfaces.publicStatus: No ThumbGate entry found in the current README search.
surfaces.operatorUse: Treat this as a clean add-listing submission, not a rename repair.
surfaces.evidenceSummary: README search returned no `thumbgate` or `IgorGanapolsky` matches, so this surface is still missing entirely.
surfaces.nextRepair: Open a new listing PR with ThumbGate-only copy and the canonical GitHub repository.

followOnOffers.label: Proof-backed setup guide
followOnOffers.buyer: Directory visitors who want a current install and proof surface before anything sales-led.

followOnOffers.label: ThumbGate Pro
followOnOffers.buyer: Directory visitors who already want the self-serve path and need a tracked paid-intent lane after the guide.

followOnOffers.label: Workflow Hardening Sprint
followOnOffers.buyer: Teams that already named one repeated workflow failure and want rollout proof, not just a directory listing.

operatorQueue.evidence: Search for `thumbgate` still resolves to `IgorGanapolsky/mcp-memory-gateway`, which leaks the retired product identity into a major MCP registry.
operatorQueue.proofTrigger: Do this before sending more discovery traffic into Glama because the current slug and summary still encode legacy positioning.
operatorQueue.recommendedMotion: Claim or edit the Glama listing so the slug, summary, and repo link are ThumbGate-only.

operatorQueue.evidence: Smithery search returns the legacy `rlhf-loop/thumbgate` namespace with no canonical ThumbGate ownership path.
operatorQueue.proofTrigger: Fix before treating Smithery as a live install lane because the namespace itself is still legacy.
operatorQueue.recommendedMotion: Publish or migrate Smithery to a canonical ThumbGate namespace and retire `rlhf-loop`.

operatorQueue.evidence: The most visible awesome list already carries a live entry, but it still points to `IgorGanapolsky/mcp-memory-gateway`.
operatorQueue.proofTrigger: Repair before doing net-new list work because this is a direct naming mismatch on an already-indexed surface.
operatorQueue.recommendedMotion: Open a small README PR that swaps the repo URL to `IgorGanapolsky/ThumbGate` and preserves the pre-action gates thesis.

operatorQueue.evidence: No current ThumbGate entry exists in the appcypher list, so this is clean acquisition expansion instead of repair.
operatorQueue.proofTrigger: Only pursue after the legacy-name repairs are underway so new discovery traffic sees one canonical identity.
operatorQueue.recommendedMotion: Open a new listing PR with ThumbGate-only copy, the npm package, and the canonical GitHub repository.

operatorQueue.evidence: MCP.so already exposes the correct ThumbGate slug and current overview, so it can anchor every other repair.
operatorQueue.proofTrigger: Use as the backstop whenever a directory repair needs a live canonical listing reference.
operatorQueue.recommendedMotion: Preserve this listing as the canonical reference and mirror its ThumbGate-only naming everywhere else.

outreachDrafts.draft: ThumbGate currently appears in Glama search under the legacy `IgorGanapolsky/mcp-memory-gateway` slug even though the active repository, npm package, and public launch surface are all `ThumbGate`. Please update the slug and summary so the listing points to `IgorGanapolsky/ThumbGate` and uses ThumbGate-only copy.

The current Smithery search result for `thumbgate` resolves to the legacy `rlhf-loop/thumbgate` namespace. The active package and repository are `thumbgate` and `IgorGanapolsky/ThumbGate`. Publish or migrate the listing under the canonical ThumbGate namespace before treating Smithery as a live acquisition lane.

This PR updates the ThumbGate entry from the retired `IgorGanapolsky/mcp-memory-gateway` repository to the active `IgorGanapolsky/ThumbGate` repository. The description remains focused on ThumbGate as pre-action gates that prevent AI coding agents from repeating known mistakes.

This PR adds ThumbGate to the list using the canonical repository and current product language. ThumbGate is the pre-action gates layer for AI coding agents: it captures explicit feedback, turns repeated failures into prevention rules, and blocks repeat mistakes before risky actions run again.

measurementPlan.policy: Treat directory presence as acquisition evidence only after a tracked guide click, install-surface click, or qualified workflow conversation exists.
measurementPlan.minimumUsefulSignal: One tracked setup-guide visit or workflow-sprint conversation sourced from a repaired directory surface.
measurementPlan.strongSignal: Three tracked paid-intent events sourced from repaired directory referrals across guide, Pro, or sprint lanes.
guardrails: Do not claim directory approval, ranking, installs, or revenue without direct command evidence.
Do not ship new directory copy that mentions `mcp-memory-gateway`, `rlhf-loop`, or other retired product names as active surfaces.
Keep proof claims aligned with VERIFICATION_EVIDENCE.md.

milestones.goal: Repair legacy naming on Glama, Smithery, and the highest-reach awesome list before broadening directory distribution.
milestones.decisionRule: Do not add lower-priority directories until the visible legacy-name leaks are fixed or actively queued.

milestones.goal: Measure whether repaired directory referrals produce guide clicks or qualified workflow conversations.
milestones.decisionRule: If referral clicks exist without paid intent, move proof and install CTAs higher on the linked destination pages.

milestones.goal: Prune low-signal directories and keep only the surfaces that produce tracked downstream intent.
milestones.decisionRule: If a directory does not create tracked guide clicks or workflow conversations, stop treating it as an active acquisition lane.

doNotCountAsSuccess: directory pages that still use legacy names
directory visibility without a tracked downstream click
unverified claims about official registry presence, approval, or paid traffic
